metadata.description:
This paper fully closes the unified quintic on one theorem-bearing surface inside lifted Phase Calculus. The target is the quintic problem carried in lifted state rather than discharged through scalar radicals.

The base carried state is

$$Ξ = (A, q, θ, κ, c)$$

where $q = (u, v)$ and $c$ is the germ interval

$$[θ − π/(u v), θ + π/(u v)]$$

The branch-complete transport state is

$$ΞP = (A, q, θ, κ, c, s, h)$$

where $s$ marks the active sheet and $h$ retains full branch history.

The paper proves four structural facts. Visible projection is not state-complete. The $Q⁴$ memory word preserves visible projection while incrementing completed-turn memory. Balanced refinement $B(u, v) = sort(v, u + v)$ generates the Fibonacci corridor, reaches the anchor $(55, 89)$, and locks the edge residual at exactly $1/24$. Lifted sheet commutators remain nontrivial yet stay invisible to visible projection.

It then establishes the native Red-filter quotient entirely inside Phase Calculus: $Π_Red$ composed with $B$ equals $G_Red$ composed with $Π_Red$, where $Π_Red$ of $Ξ$ projects to $q = (u, v)$ and $G_Red(u, v) = sort(v, u + v)$.

The scalar descendants on this surface are the Liouvillian coordinate $ρ(u, v) = log(u v)$ and the exact packet-collapse scalar $R(u, v) = exp(2πi/(u v))$. No exp-minus-log operator is imported.

This strengthens the quotient in three exact ways: a state-complete Red simulation lower bound, native projector-preserved invariants on the positive corridor, and a complete worked packet-collapse descent at the canonical anchor.

The generic irreducible quintic lies outside finite scalar Red discharge for the same reason it lies outside finite radical expressions: its monodromy group is S5, the derived series terminates at the perfect group A5, and the native scalar coordinate remains Liouvillian.

The release ships with the practical utility lifted_quintic_certifier.py. This certifier delivers all five roots of the Bring quintic $x^5 − x + 1$ at depth 21 with half-widths below $10^{-8}$ and projected polynomial residuals at machine precision.

The full release — paper, Lean formalization, SymPy validation audit, JSON certificates, and certifier — forms one completely closed, self-contained artifact.

references.reference: Lietz, J. (2026). Phase Calculus - Lifted-State Operator Calculus of the Primitive Roll with Connection Closure and Shadow/Completion Branch (v11.1). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19491529
Lietz, J. (2026). Operational Utility and Multiscale Invariance of Phase Calculus: A Discrete Foundational Grammar for Dynamics (v0.1). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19521030
Lietz, J. (2026). CF19: The Full Lifted Object of the Primitive Roll and Exact Return to Lifted Origin (v0.1). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19267927

description.en:
SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Neuroca-PRL-2.1
Copyright © 2025 Justin K. Lietz, Neuroca, Inc. All Rights Reserved.

NOTE: This LICENSE regards the Void Dynamics Model runtime, not the physics, mathematics, or standalone theorems unless otherwise stated. By default the CC BY-NC-SA SA Attribution-NC-ShareAlike license holds. This license allows anyone to share and adapt non-commercially; must use the same license.

This repository uses a proprietary dual-license to promote open academic and personal (non-commercial) use,
while ensuring commercial applications align with ethical standards and protect proprietary inventions.


Neuroca Proprietary Dual License (Academic + Commercial)
Version: 2.1  
Last updated: November 11, 2025

Copyright © 2025  
Justin K. Lietz & Neuroca, Inc.  
All Rights Reserved.

Contact: 📩 justin@neuroca.ai

---

0) Overview

Intent and Scope. Licensor promotes open academic research and personal, non-commercial use of the Materials
while requiring a separate commercial license for any monetized or for-profit use, and protecting proprietary
inventions and pending patents. This dual-license applies worldwide unless a different governing law is specified.

Acceptance. By accessing, downloading, or using the repository or any materials, you agree to be bound by these
Academic License terms solely for Academic Use and Personal Use, as defined. Commercial Use is strictly prohibited
without a separate, duly executed commercial license agreement with Licensor. If you do not agree, do not use the Materials.

---

1) Dual-License Model

- Academic License (non-commercial) — includes Personal Use; permitted without written permission, subject to
attribution, share-alike for academic derivatives, and ethical restrictions.
- Commercial License — required for any Commercial Use, including monetized, for-profit, hosted/SaaS, or
proprietary-integrating use. Request via `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE_REQUEST.md`.

Summary of permitted Academic/Personal uses includes open research, teaching, coursework, theses/dissertations,
non-commercial reproducibility and benchmarking, and personal non-commercial exploration.

Summary of uses requiring a Commercial License includes product/service integration, fee-based activities, internal
corporate R&D leading to proprietary outcomes or patents, hosted or SaaS offerings, consulting/training for a fee,
technology transfer, sublicensing, or monetization of outputs derived from the Materials.

Third-Party Licenses. Third-party components obtained via package managers remain under their respective licenses.
See Section 6 for how third-party licenses interact with this dual license and for notice/compliance obligations.

---

2) Definitions

- Academic Use — use for research, education, teaching, coursework, theses/dissertations, non-commercial open research,
reproducibility/benchmarking, and publication of results. Includes research funded by commercial entities only if
the results and any derivative works are published openly and are not made proprietary, and if such use does not involve
monetization or confer a commercial advantage beyond access to openly published results.
- Personal Use — individual, non-commercial use by a natural person on systems personally controlled by that person,
without revenue generation, fees, or monetization of outputs, services, or derivative works, and without performance for
or within a for-profit entity.
- Commercial Use — any use involving revenue, fees, or commercial advantage; any use for/within a for-profit entity or
under a non-academic government contract; fee-based consulting/training/managed services; technology transfer, sublicensing,
or licensing programs; integration into products or services; internal corporate R&D leading to proprietary outcomes or
patents; SaaS/hosted/cloud services exposing/embedding/integrating/deriving functionality from the Materials; or monetization
of outputs, models, or derivative works based on the Materials.
- Derivative Work — any work based on, adapted from, incorporating, or modified from the repository or the materials,
including code, data, designs, equations, parameterizations, tools, models, experiments, documentation, or combinations.
- Reference-Based Use — a re-implementation or work substantially guided by methods, equations, parameter values,
architectural choices, experimental procedures, training regimes, or analysis techniques disclosed in the repository or
materials (excluding purely independent implementations developed without material reliance).
- Outputs — any results, models, artifacts, visualizations, datasets, logs, analyses, publications, or other content
generated using or derived from the Materials, including those produced by Academic Derivative Works or Reference-Based Use.
- Repository — the GitHub repository at https://github.com/justinlietz93/Prometheus_VDM and official branches/releases
as maintained by Licensor.
- Academic Derivative Works — Derivative Works created and used solely for Academic Use or Personal Use in compliance
with this Academic License.
- Licensee / you — the individual or entity exercising rights under this License.

---

3) Academic License (Non-Commercial; includes Personal)

3.1 Grant of Rights
Subject to the terms herein, Licensor grants you a non-exclusive, worldwide, non-transferable, non-sublicensable,
royalty-free license to use, reproduce, and create Academic Derivative Works of the Materials solely for Academic Use
and Personal Use.

3.2 Permitted Uses (illustrative)
- Academic research, teaching, coursework, and theses/dissertations.
- Non-commercial open research, reproducibility, and benchmarking activities.
- Personal non-commercial exploration and learning by individuals.
- Sponsored research by commercial entities only if outcomes remain non-proprietary and are published openly
under these Academic terms, with no monetization or commercial advantage beyond access to openly published results.

3.3 Mandatory Attribution (use, replication, rework, or reference)
You must provide credit to Licensor in any Academic Use, Personal Use, publication, release of Academic Derivative Works,
or Reference-Based Use. Include the following citation:

Citation:  
Lietz, Justin K.; Neuroca, Inc. (2025). Prometheus_VDM (VDM): Neuroca Proprietary Dual License (Academic + Commercial) v2.1.
GitHub repository: https://github.com/justinlietz93/Prometheus_VDM.

BibTeX:
```bibtex
@misc{Prometheus_VDM_2025,
author = {Justin K. Lietz and Neuroca, Inc.},
title = {Prometheus_VDM (VDM): Neuroca Proprietary Dual License (Academic + Commercial) v2.1},
howpublished = {GitHub repository},
year = {2025},
url = {https://github.com/justinlietz93/Prometheus_VDM}
}
```

3.4 Share-Alike for Academic Derivative Works
Academic Derivative Works must be distributed, if distributed, under terms no less protective and no more permissive than
this Academic License, including the non-commercial restriction and the attribution requirement, or under substantially
similar non-commercial, attribution-required terms. This share-alike requirement does not grant rights for Commercial Use.

3.5 Outputs Under Academic/Personal Use
Outputs generated under Academic or Personal Use may be used and shared non-commercially, with required attribution,
and with no monetization.

3.6 Prohibited Uses Under Academic/Personal Use
- Any Commercial Use (see §4).
- Paid consulting, fee-based training, managed services, or similar activities.
- Technology transfer, sublicensing, or licensing to commercial entities.

3.7 Ethical Restrictions
- No use for weapons development, mass surveillance, or applications intended for harm, exploitation, or unlawful discrimination.
- Work on AI safety, interpretability, and alignment is encouraged.

Compliance tip. Internal corporate R&D is deemed Commercial Use unless all resulting outputs, derivatives, and methods are
published openly and non-proprietarily under these Academic terms without monetization and without conferring a commercial
advantage.

---

4) Commercial License

4.1 Commercial Scope (non-exhaustive examples)
- Integration into any product or service (software or hardware), including SaaS or hosted functionality.
- Fee-based consulting, training, managed services, or platform access.
- Internal corporate or non-academic government R&D leading to proprietary outcomes or patents.
- Monetization of Outputs, models, or derivative works derived from the Materials.
- Technology transfer, sublicensing, IP packaging, or licensing programs.

4.2 Prohibition Without License
No Commercial Use is permitted without prior written permission under a separate, signed commercial license agreement with
Licensor.

4.3 Request Process
To request a commercial license, complete `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE_REQUEST.md` and email justin@neuroca.ai.

4.4 Note
Commercial license terms, negotiated separately, may address rights to monetize outputs, redistribution, embedding,
cloud/SaaS rights, user or seat limits, support/updates, audit rights, termination, and patent license terms.

---

5) Patent Notice & Proprietary Inventions

The Materials may disclose or embody proprietary inventions and patent-pending technologies, including:
- RE-VGSP — Resonance-Enhanced Valence-Gated Synaptic Plasticity
- SIE — Self-Improvement Engine
- EHTP — Emergent Hierarchical Topology Probe
- GDSP — Goal-Directed Structural Plasticity
- Multi-Phase Training Strategies — Curriculum/homeostatic stimuli modulation
- Hierarchical TDA Analysis — Real-time knowledge-graph diagnostics
- Self-Modulating Neuron Models — Adaptive intrinsic plasticity
- Proprietary Equations and Parameterizations

Patent Licensing Position. The Academic License grants rights to use provided artifacts for Academic Use only.
No patent license for Commercial Use is granted. Any Commercial Use of covered methods requires a separate commercial
license expressly granting applicable patent rights.

Defensive Termination. If you or your affiliate assert a patent claim alleging that the Materials, Licensor contributions,
or implementations infringe any patent, all rights granted under this License automatically terminate as of the filing date.

---

6) Third-Party Licenses & Exceptions

6.1 Scope Control and File Headers
File-level headers indicate authorship/origin. If a file lacks a header, this License applies to Neuroca-authored files by default.

6.2 Dependencies via Package Managers
Third-party dependencies installed via pip or other package managers remain under their own licenses and are not re-licensed
by this License. Your use must comply with those licenses.

6.3 Vendored Third-Party Code
If third-party code is ever vendored:
- Third-party files retain their original license headers and are placed under `third_party/<project>/` with original LICENSE/NOTICE preserved.
- For mixed-origin files, the original license governs the third-party portions; Neuroca modifications are licensed under this dual license
to the extent permitted by the upstream license.

6.4 Precedence and Compatibility
Licensor respects upstream license prohibitions on adding further restrictions to derivatives. Aggregation or import of libraries does not
change their licenses; copying or modifying code must honor upstream conditions.

6.5 Notice Obligations
Preserve required notices (e.g., MPL-2.0, Apache-2.0) when distributing modified files or binaries. See `NOTICE.md` for acknowledgments.

6.6 Required File Headers (templates)
Neuroca-Authored Files
```
SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Neuroca-PRL-2.1
Copyright © 2025 Justin K. Lietz, Neuroca, Inc.
Licensed under the Neuroca Proprietary Dual License (Academic + Commercial). See LICENSE.md for terms.
```

Third-Party or Mixed-Origin Files
```
This file includes or is derived from third-party material.
Original license and notices apply to those portions (see third_party/ and NOTICE.md).
Neuroca modifications (if any) © 2025 Justin K. Lietz, Neuroca, Inc., licensed under LicenseRef-Neuroca-PRL-2.1
to the extent permitted by the original license. See LICENSE.md and third_party/ files.
```

---

7) Enforcement

Academic/Personal Violations include using Academic terms for Commercial Use, failing to provide required attribution,
or using the Materials in prohibited ethical domains.

Commercial Violations include unlicensed Commercial Use, unauthorized sublicensing or redistribution, or concealment/misrepresentation
of proprietary methods or derivatives derived from the Materials.

Remedies. Upon breach, all rights granted terminate immediately. Licensor reserves all rights and remedies available at law or in equity,
including claims for copyright infringement, trade secret misappropriation, and patent infringement.

---

8) Warranty, Liability, Termination

Disclaimer. THE MATERIALS ARE PROVIDED “AS IS” AND “AS AVAILABLE” WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED,
INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT. YOUR USE IS AT YOUR SOLE RISK.

Limitation of Liability. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, LICENSOR SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL,
CONSEQUENTIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR ANY LOSS OF PROFITS, REVENUE, DATA, OR GOODWILL. IN NO EVENT SHALL LICENSOR’S AGGREGATE
LIABILITY EXCEED ONE HUNDRED U.S. DOLLARS (US$100).

Termination. This License and all rights granted terminate automatically upon your breach. Sections relating to attribution, proprietary rights,
patents, third-party licenses, warranty disclaimers, limitations of liability, enforcement, and termination survive termination.

---

9) Miscellaneous

- Governing Law and Venue. [Optional: insert governing law and exclusive jurisdiction/venue.]
- Export Controls. You will comply with applicable export control and sanctions laws and will not export/re-export the Materials in violation thereof.
- Trademarks. No rights to use Licensor’s names, logos, or marks (“Neuroca,” “Prometheus_VDM,” “VDM,” “Void Nexus”), except factual references without
implication of sponsorship/endorsement. See `TRADEMARKS.md` if provided.
- Entire Agreement. This License constitutes the entire agreement concerning the Materials under the Academic License. Commercial licensing is governed
exclusively by a separate, signed agreement.
- Severability. If any provision is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain in full force and effect.
- No Waiver. No failure or delay by Licensor in exercising any right operates as a waiver.
- Updates. Licensor may publish updated versions from time to time. Updates do not retroactively alter rights previously granted unless you agree.

---

10) How to Request a License or Clarification

- Commercial. Complete `COMMERCIAL_LICENSE_REQUEST.md` and email justin@neuroca.ai with subject “Commercial License Request – VDM / Neuroca.”
- Academic/Personal Clarifications. For gray areas or written confirmation of non-commercial uses, complete `ACADEMIC_LICENSE_REQUEST.md` and email
justin@neuroca.ai with subject “Academic/Personal Clarification – VDM / Neuroca.”

metadata.title: Unified Quintic: Lifted Quintic Transport, Red-Filter Quotient Descent, and Immediate Root Certification

metadata.curation_policy:
This community enforces a strict, curiosity driven, falsification-first curation standard. The goal is not to accumulate records. The goal is to ensure that every record in this archive is auditable, reproducible, and built to be attacked.

Scope. Records accepted into this community must fall within one or more of the following categories: (1) theorem-bearing manuscripts within the VDM derivation tree, (2) adversarial validation artifacts attacking published VDM claims, (3) empirical analyses of the VDM cognitive runtime, (4) instrumentation or calibration records supporting VDM research, (5) code repository releases with provenance documentation, (6) formal critique reports or independent reproduction attempts.

Minimum requirements for all records.

Every record must include a plain-English abstract stating what is claimed. Every primary claim must be explicitly stated, uniquely identified, and paired with a decisive metric, a falsifier, its assumption dependence, and a scope classification. No claim may hide behind "standard argument," "it is well known," "follows similarly," or "checked numerically" without stating exactly what is being delegated and why. Every assumption must be explicit, scoped, and typed. Every record must state what it does not prove.

Publication bundle standard (Complete Formalisms and theorem-bearing manuscripts).

A Complete Formalism publication is not complete when it merely states claims. It is complete only when its full formal burden is attacked across all required artifact layers: paper, Lean 4, SymPy, Jupyter, and manifest.

The paper must carry the full load-bearing formal burden: definitions, theorems, derivation chains, operator relations, assumption boundaries, falsifiers, and scope limits.

Lean 4 must prove every exact formalizable statement. Any omission must be classified with an explicit reason (non-formalizable, numerical, symbolic, or deferred).

SymPy must attack every symbolic identity and operator law and terminate with an explicit FINAL_RESULT: PASS or FINAL_RESULT: FAIL.

Jupyter must attack every numerical, geometric, statistical, and gate-facing consequence in reviewer-readable form, including negative controls, explicit residuals, labeled thresholds, and a final coverage ledger.

A README must map every burden to its artifact location with exact run instructions and expected outputs. SHA-256 hashes must cover all bundled artifacts.

Review standard. This community uses the CRAFT/BASS framework (Credibility, Rigor, Assumption, Falsifiability, Terminology / Bayesian Audit of Scientific Substance) for internal review. Records targeting a BASS score below 70 will not be accepted unless explicitly labeled as draft, partial, or in-progress.

What will be rejected. Records that contain marketing language, vague claims without falsification criteria, omitted negative results, non-runnable validation artifacts, or claims asserted as validated without adversarial attack will be rejected or returned for revision.

Labeling. Every record must carry one of the following statuses: validated (full bundle passes all gates), draft (incomplete validation, explicitly labeled), partial (some burdens unresolved, explicitly enumerated), or falsified (a published claim has been disproven, with the correction documented).

No record may be described as "validated" unless its full bundle satisfies the publication validation requirements.

External contributions. Independent researchers who wish to submit reproduction attempts, critique reports, or adversarial analyses targeting records in this community are welcome. Contact justin@neuroca.ai before submission.
metadata.description: This is a branch community off of the larger collective of the Void Dynamics Model community created specifically to organize the research related only to the mathematical framework of Phase Calculus and its operational utility.
metadata.page:
The Void Dynamics Model (VDM) is an independent research program operated by Neuroca, Inc. (Neenah, WI) under principal investigator Justin K. Lietz.

VDM has two tracks.

Track 1 — Physics. A unified framework deriving quantum geometry, thermodynamics, gauge structure, gravity, and cosmology from a single primitive bifurcation invariant via metriplectic geometry, bifurcation theory, and domain wall dynamics. The formal spine is a derivation tree of Complete Formalisms (CF000–CF19), each carrying its own theorem surface, falsification gates, and explicit scope boundaries.

Track 2 — Cognitive Runtime. A zero-training, physics-gated cognitive architecture exploring how conservation-vs.-dissipation dynamics produce stable, adaptive behavior in sparse, event-driven neural graphs — no backpropagation, no training data, no gradient descent.

This community regards the Phase Calculus framework which works interchangeably between those tracks.

Governing principle. Every record in this community is built to be proven wrong. Predictions are preregistered and time-stamped before results are collected. Claims carry explicit pass/fail gates. Negative controls and failure reports ship by default. If a result breaks, we publish the break.

What this community contains:

  • Theorem-bearing manuscripts (Complete Formalisms, Phase Calculus, universality papers, standalone theorems, cogitants)
  • Adversarial validation bundles (Lean 4 packages, SymPy scripts, skeptical-reviewer Jupyter notebooks, SHA-256 manifests)
  • Cognitive runtime publications (empirical analyses, complexity metric instruments, runtime architectures)
  • Instrumentation and calibration records
  • Code repository releases with cryptographic provenance

Open challenge. Attempts to falsify any record in this community are encouraged. If your critique successfully disproves a published claim and we can verify your results, we will publish a formal follow-up conceding the original claim and giving you full credit for the correction. Contact: justin@neuroca.ai

Resources:

  • Website: https://www.neuroca.ai
  • ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0008-9028-1366
  • Academia.edu: https://independent.academia.edu/justinlietz
  • GitHub (Primitive Bifurcation Law): https://github.com/justinlietz93/primitive_bifurcation_law_VDM
  • GitHub (Prometheus VDM): https://github.com/justinlietz93/Prometheus_VDM
metadata.title: Void Dynamics Model: Phase Calculus

metadata.description:
One bifurcation invariant.
Background-independent metriplectic field theory + zero-training cognitive runtime across 20 Complete Formalisms.
Adversarial Lean 4/SymPy/Jupyter bundles and pass/fail gates with every record. 
Built to be attacked.
